entries.titleEn: What the Kleinunternehmer (small business) scheme means
questionExamples: what can I do as a Kleinunternehmer
explain the small business scheme
do I charge VAT as a Kleinunternehmer
what are the Kleinunternehmer turnover limits
entries.answerEn: As a Kleinunternehmer under §19 UStG your sales are VAT-exempt: you don't charge VAT on your invoices and can't deduct input VAT. It applies if your net turnover stayed under €25,000 in the previous calendar year and doesn't exceed €100,000 in the current one (rules in force since 2025). Your invoices must note the exemption (a plain-language reference is enough), and you still file an annual income-tax return. For whether it's the right choice for your situation, a Taxfix tax expert can advise.

entries.titleEn: What reverse charge means on an invoice
questionExamples: what does reverse charge mean
why is there no VAT on my EU invoice
reverse charge wording on invoice
entries.answerEn: Reverse charge means the recipient — not you — accounts for the VAT. For a general B2B service to a business in another EU country, the place of supply is the customer's country (§3a(2) UStG), so you invoice without German VAT and add the wording “Steuerschuldnerschaft des Leistungsempfängers” (reverse charge, §13b/§14a UStG), showing both parties' VAT IDs. You then usually report it in a recapitulative statement (Zusammenfassende Meldung).

questionExamples: what is the Zusammenfassende Meldung
do I have to report EU sales
what is a ZM report
entries.answerEn: The Zusammenfassende Meldung (ZM, §18a UStG) is a periodic report you file for intra-EU B2B services where the recipient owes the VAT under reverse charge. It lists each customer's VAT ID and the amount, and is filed electronically via ELSTER separately from your VAT return.

entries.titleEn: What must appear on a German invoice
questionExamples: what must a German invoice contain
mandatory invoice fields
what info goes on an invoice
entries.answerEn: Under §14 UStG an invoice must show: your full name and address and the customer's; your tax number or VAT ID; the issue date; a sequential invoice number; the quantity and type of the service; the time of supply; the net amount; and the VAT rate and amount — or a reference to the exemption if none is charged (e.g. Kleinunternehmer or reverse charge).

questionExamples: what is the German VAT rate
how much VAT do I charge
standard VAT percentage
entries.answerEn: The German standard VAT rate is 19% of the net amount (§12(1) UStG), which applies to ordinary professional services. A reduced 7% rate exists for specific categories that this prototype doesn't cover. Whether a reduced rate or exemption applies to your specific service is best confirmed with a tax expert.

entries.topic: Tax number vs VAT ID vs Steuer-ID
entries.titleEn: The different German tax numbers explained
questionExamples: difference between tax number and VAT ID
what is my Steuer-ID
tax number vs VAT ID
which number goes on my invoice
entries.answerEn: There are three different numbers. The Steuer-Identifikationsnummer (Steuer-ID) is your lifelong personal tax id, assigned by the BZSt (§139a AO). The Steuernummer is issued by your local tax office for your business filings. The USt-IdNr. (VAT ID) is a separate number from the BZSt for intra-EU trade. On invoices you show your tax number or VAT ID; the VAT ID is required for EU B2B reverse-charge invoices.

entries.titleEn: How to get a German VAT ID
questionExamples: how do I get a VAT ID
where do I apply for a USt-IdNr
do I need a VAT ID
entries.answerEn: The USt-IdNr. (VAT ID) is issued by the Bundeszentralamt für Steuern (BZSt), separately from your tax number. You need it if you trade goods or services B2B within the EU. You can request it online at the BZSt or, for a new business, directly on the tax registration questionnaire; it is issued only after your VAT registration at the tax office and sent by post.

entries.titleEn: How to register a freelance activity
questionExamples: how do I register as a freelancer
how do I start freelancing in Germany
what is the Fragebogen zur steuerlichen Erfassung
entries.answerEn: When you start a self-employed/freelance activity you must notify the competent tax office within one month (§138 AO) — commercial businesses notify the municipality (Gewerbeanmeldung) instead. You do this through the “Fragebogen zur steuerlichen Erfassung” (tax registration questionnaire), submitted via ELSTER. Based on it, the tax office issues your Steuernummer and sets your VAT obligations. For which category fits your activity, a tax expert can help.

entries.titleEn: What ELSTER is and what it's for
questionExamples: what is ELSTER
how do I file taxes online
how do I submit a VAT return
entries.answerEn: ELSTER (Mein ELSTER) is the German tax administration's free online portal for submitting tax returns and VAT advance returns electronically and authenticated. Freelancers use it to file the registration questionnaire, monthly/quarterly VAT advance returns, the recapitulative statement, and annual returns. You need a free ELSTER user account (certificate) to sign in.

entries.titleEn: How often you file VAT returns
questionExamples: how often do I file VAT returns
monthly or quarterly VAT
when is my VAT return due
entries.answerEn: VAT advance returns (Voranmeldungen) are filed via ELSTER by the 10th day after each period (§18 UStG). The period is quarterly by default, monthly if last year's VAT was over €9,000, and the tax office may waive filing if it was €2,000 or less; new businesses file monthly in the founding year and the next. For your exact schedule and dates, check your tax office notice or ask a tax expert.

entries.titleEn: The difference between income tax and VAT
questionExamples: difference between income tax and VAT
do I pay income tax and VAT
how are freelancers taxed
entries.answerEn: They're two separate things. VAT (Umsatzsteuer) is a tax you collect on your sales and pass to the tax office via advance returns — it doesn't reduce your income. Income tax (Einkommensteuer) is charged on your annual profit (income minus expenses) via your income-tax return. This prototype focuses on VAT/invoicing; for your income-tax position and amounts, a Taxfix tax expert can help.
